verdicts.gptExplanation: Mies van der Rohe, Marcel Breuer and Herbert Bayer are both Bauhaus masters and creators/designers of iconic Bauhaus works, creating possible cross-links with the works group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Acceptable if intended, but cleaner easy puzzles avoid creator-work pairings across groups.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Spitzweg is usually associated with Biedermeier rather than straightforward Romanticism, and Feuerbach is not a clean Realism fit in the same everyday sense as Menzel or Leibl.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more canonical movement examples or broaden the labels.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Deutsches Historisches and DHM Berlin refer to the same museum, creating a duplicate within the Geschichtsmuseen group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep one and replace the other with another history museum.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Verkehrshaus is in Lucerne, Switzerland, not Germany, so it does not fit 'Museen Deutschland'.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a German transport/technology museum such as Verkehrsmuseum Dresden or Technik Museum Sinsheim.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Deutsches Museum can be both a major technology museum and a broader science/natural-history-adjacent institution, but the bigger issue is that several entries are abbreviated/generic rather than unique museum names.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use full unique museum names.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sanssouci is primarily Rococo rather than Baroque, so its placement under Barock is debatable for an easy architecture puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a clearer Baroque building or label the group Barock/Rokoko.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Meßberg is a location associated with Kontorhausviertel, while Chilehaus, Sprinkenhof and Mohlenhof are buildings. It is not parallel and can also fit broader historic Hamburg geography.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Meßberg with a specific Kontorhaus such as Montanhof.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Speicherstadt items and Kontorhäuser are both historic Hamburg architecture/UNESCO-area clues, and 'Roter Backsteinbau' is a generic feature that can describe several Kontorhäuser too.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more specific Speicherstadt landmarks or rename the group to architectural features.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Frauenkirche is Gothic, not Münchner Barock, so it is misplaced.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Asamkirche, Bürgersaalkirche, Schloss Schleißheim or another clear Baroque item.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Elvira-Atelier and Hofatelier Elvira are the same building/name, creating a duplicate in the Jugendstil group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep one and replace the other with another Munich Jugendstil building.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Preysing-Palais is a Rococo/Baroque palace, not Jugendstil.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a clear Jugendstil site such as Ainmillerstraße facades or Jugendstilhaus examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Reichstag predates the Kaiserreich's end but is also a central modern/historic Berlin landmark; Humboldtforum is a reconstruction of the Berlin Palace and could be read as historic as well as modern.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer period labels or replace ambiguous landmark items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Schloss Charlottenburg is Baroque/Prussian and not a Kaiserzeit building.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a clearer Kaiserzeit building such as Kaiser-Wilhelm-Gedächtniskirche or Bode-Museum.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Weber and Schumann appear as Romantic music items, while Weber is also strongly associated with German opera in nearby cultural knowledge; not unfair by itself, but the category is broad and less concrete than the building/painting/literature groups.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use full names or works to reduce ambiguity.

verdicts.gptExplanation: AEG-Turbinenhalle is a landmark of early modern industrial architecture, not Jugendstil.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Mathildenhöhe, Secessionsgebäude, Majolikahaus, or another Jugendstil/Art Nouveau building.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Elvira Atelier and Müllersches Volksbad also appear in the Munich architecture puzzle as Jugendstil München; that is fine across puzzles, but within this puzzle 'Villa Stuck' is both a building and tied to Franz von Stuck in the painter group, creating a creator/place cross-link.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Avoid pairing the building and its artist in separate groups if this causes solver pull.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Smartfabric and Recycelfiber are awkward English/neo-English terms in a German easy puzzle and may not be familiar to the target audience.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use German or established terms such as Smart Textiles, Recyclingfasern, nachhaltige Mode, digitale Kleidung.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Nachhaltigkeit belongs just as much to future fashion as current fashion, and 3D-Druck already exists in current fashion; the present/future split is fuzzy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use time periods with unambiguous historical styles or avoid speculative future categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several brand-category placements are questionable: Trigema is not streetwear, Steiff is mainly plush toys rather than an accessories fashion brand, and Birkenstock is footwear rather than an accessories brand.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer German fashion/accessory labels or rename categories to match the included brands.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sportswear, streetwear, luxury and accessories brands overlap in fashion retail; Adidas/Puma can be streetwear, Hugo Boss can include accessories, and MCM is a luxury/accessories brand.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more objective labels such as founded city, product type, or brand founders.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Schmied and Schmieden are the profession and technique form of the same root, and Hämmern/Schleifen can apply across many crafts, so professions and techniques overlap semantically.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Avoid same-root profession/technique pairs or make all techniques specific and distinct.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Zünfte and traditionelle Berufe overlap because Bäckerzunft, Fleischerzunft, Schuhmacherzunft and Zimmerzunft directly correspond to trades/professions.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use guild symbols, tools, or products instead of guild names matching professions.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Keramik-Stile mixes materials/types (Steingut, Steinzeug, Porzellan, Terrakotta), while Porzellan also strongly points to Meißen in the places group. The style/type/place categories are not clean for easy solvers.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to 'Keramikarten' and avoid including a place whose fame is defined by one listed type, or use simpler object/tool categories.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Kandern and Bunzlau are relatively obscure ceramic places for easy tier compared with Meißen.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more widely known places or raise the tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Glasprodukte and Glas-Anwendungen overlap: Trinkglas is a glass product, and Linse/Optik and Glasfaser are both application/product-like technical uses. The distinction is not stable.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Separate into concrete household products and industrial/scientific applications without borderline items, or replace one group.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Bohemia is English/Latinized; German puzzle language would normally use Böhmen. Murano is also outside Germany, which is fine only if the category is international glass places.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Böhmen, and clarify if the group is international rather than German.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Nieten“ can mean the technique of riveting, but also rivets as physical metal items/products. In a puzzle that also has „Metallprodukte“, this is not fully unambiguous.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer technique such as „Bohren“ or „Walzen“, or change the item to „Vernieten“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The categories „Webtechniken“, „Textilien“ and „Textil-Muster“ overlap semantically. „Damast“ and „Jacquard“ are not just simple visual patterns; they are tied to weave structures/techniques and can also name fabrics.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: For an easy puzzle, use plain visual patterns only, e.g. „Streifen, Karo, Punkte, Blumenmuster“, and avoid weave/fabric terms.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Faden“ and „Garn“ are very close everyday terms and may be treated as near-synonyms by solvers, especially at easy tier.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace one with a more distinct textile item such as „Band“, „Filz“ or „Spitze“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Leinen“ is commonly the fabric/material made from flax, not the fiber itself. As a „Faserart“, „Flachs“ would be clearer.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace „Leinen“ with „Flachs“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Leinwandbindung“, „Köperbindung“ and „Atlasbindung“ are specialist textile terms and feel too technical for an easy everyday German puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more concrete textile actions such as „Weben“, „Nähen“, „Stricken“, „Sticken“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Bundeswirtschaftsrat“ is not a standard German economic institution on the level of Bundesbank, IHK or Ifo-Institut. It is likely to confuse solvers or be perceived as invented/incorrect.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a clearer institution such as „Bundeskartellamt“, „Wirtschaftsministerium“ or „DIHK“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: This puzzle is heavy with institutions, abbreviations and macroeconomic concepts. For easy tier, „BIP“, „IHK“, „Ifo-Institut“, „Tarifvertrag“ and „Mittelstand“ are relatively abstract compared with concrete everyday categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to medium or simplify with more everyday items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Frankfurt“ and „Hamburg“ are not especially characteristic easy-recognition German Maschinenbau locations in the way Stuttgart is. The group feels arbitrary and weakly anchored.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more clearly associated industrial locations or replace the whole category with a more concrete one.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Maschinenbau-Produkte“ and „Maschinenbau-Branchen“ overlap: „Werkzeugbau“ and „Fördertechnik“ can be understood as product/technology areas within machinery rather than cleanly separate concepts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Make one category concrete products only and the other clearly company/region/person categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Boehringer“ is not normally used alone as the company name; the recognizable company is „Boehringer Ingelheim“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Boehringer Ingelheim“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Pharma-Forschung“ contains process/regulatory/medical evaluation terms, not just research fields. „Zulassung“, „Wirksamkeit“ and „Nebenwirkung“ are broader pharma or medical terms, which overlaps with „Wirkstoffgruppen“ and general pharma vocabulary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to „Pharma-Begriffe“ or use clearer research items such as „Laborversuch“, „Doppelblindstudie“, „Wirkstoffsuche“, „Placebo“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Wind“ as an energy carrier and „Windrad“ as an energy technology are tightly coupled and may feel like the same concept split across groups. This is not fatal, but it weakens separation.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Windenergie“ for the carrier/source or replace one item with a less paired term.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Dessau“ is not an obvious or widely recognized German energy location at easy tier. The location category feels arbitrary compared with concrete energy companies and technologies.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more recognizable energy locations or replace the category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Ökologisch“, „Konventionell“, „Bewässerung“ and „Pflanzenschutz“ are mixed levels: the first two are farming systems, while the latter two are practices that can occur within those systems.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use one coherent concept, e.g. all farming systems or all field-work practices.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „EU-Direktzahlungen“, „Bergbauernprämie“ and „Flächenprämie“ are policy/subsidy terms that are not everyday easy-tier vocabulary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to medium or replace with concrete farm terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Bayern“ is both a tourism region and the location strongly associated with „Oktoberfest“. The categories are understandable, but this creates location/event cross-association.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace „Bayern“ with a more specific region such as „Allgäu“ or replace „Oktoberfest“ with a landmark.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Hausmannskost“ is more a type of food/cuisine than a neutral gastronomy term, so it can reasonably pull toward „Regionalküchen“ or food categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer restaurant term such as „Reservierung“, „Bedienung“ or „Rechnung“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Handelsprodukte“ is mislabeled: „Eigenmarke“, „Markenprodukt“, „Sonderangebot“ and „Aktionsware“ are product/offer types and retail concepts, overlapping with „Handelsbegriffe“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename „Handelsprodukte“ to „Angebotsarten“ or replace with actual product categories like „Milch, Brot, Waschmittel, Obst“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Verfassungsschutz“ is not a police authority; it is a domestic intelligence agency. In a „Polizeibehörden“ group, this is factually wrong.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „Zollfahndung“ only if broad law enforcement is intended, or use a police-specific authority.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Kripo“ is not simply a unit parallel to SEK/MEK/Bereitschaftspolizei; it is a police branch/department, which overlaps with police authority/organization concepts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use another clearly unit-like term or rename the category to „Polizeibereiche“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Rettungswagen“ belongs primarily to rescue/EMS rather than firefighting, though fire departments may operate ambulances. In a category specifically labeled „Feuerwehr-Fahrzeuge“, it is ambiguous.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „Rüstwagen“ or „Einsatzleitwagen“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Hydrant“ is infrastructure, not equipment carried by firefighters. It may be associated with firefighting but does not fit cleanly with Atemschutz, Schlauch and Schutzanzug.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „Helm“, „Axt“ or „Strahlrohr“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Spezialkräfte“ overlaps with branch/unit categories because KSK belongs to the Heer and KSM belongs to the Marine. The intended split is understandable but not clean.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „ABC-Abwehr“ is not generally classified as Spezialkräfte parallel to KSK/KSM/Fernspäher. It is a capability/troop type for CBRN defense.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a clearer special-operations item or rename the category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Olaf Scholz“ is not a current party chair of the SPD, so placing him under „Parteivorsitzende“ is factually wrong.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a current or historically explicit SPD party chair, or rename the category to „Bekannte Politiker“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Robert Habeck“ is not unambiguously a current party chair of Bündnis 90/Die Grünen. The item is better understood as a prominent politician than as a chairperson.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a current or historically explicit Green party chair, or rename the category to „Bekannte Politiker“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „SPD-Godesberger“ and „FDP-Neoliberalismus“ are awkward/advanced political-history clues for easy tier, and the category mixes events, ideological labels and shorthand.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Wahlsysteme“ includes „Erststimme“ and „Zweitstimme“, which are ballot/vote types rather than electoral systems. This overlaps with „Wahlprozess“ and „Wahl-Begriffe“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Gemeinderat“ and „Stadtrat“ can be offices, councils, or institutions depending on context. This overlaps with „Kommunale Institutionen“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use individual office holders only, e.g. „Bürgermeister, Kämmerer, Landrat, Beigeordneter“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Grundsicherung“ is itself a social benefit type, not just a general social term, so it overlaps with „Sozialleistungs-Arten“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move „Grundsicherung“ to benefits or replace it with a clearer term like „Einkommensprüfung“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Arztbesuch“, „Medikament“, „Krankenhaus“ and „Vorsorge“ are broad healthcare items, not specifically and cleanly „Kassenleistungen“. They overlap with general health-system vocabulary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer benefit terms such as „Impfung“, „Check-up“, „Heilmittel“, „Zahnersatz“ or rename the group.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Grundschuld“ is both an ownership/land-register concept and a financing security instrument, so it overlaps strongly with „Finanzierung“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Mietvertrag“ is a central rental term and also the basis of tenant rights, so it overlaps with „Miet-Begriffe“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer tenant-rights item such as „Mietpreisbremse“ or „Kündigungsschutz“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Servus“ is not exclusively Bavarian; it is also common in Austria and other southern German-speaking areas. It is still associated with Bavarian speech, but not uniquely.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a more specifically Bavarian item.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Tschüss“ is not specifically Plattdeutsch; it is widespread standard colloquial German. This makes the Plattdeutsch group less fair.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a more clearly Low German form.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Heim“ is standard German and not a clear Schwäbisch marker on its own.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a more recognizable Swabian word or phrase.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Dialect-specific spellings such as „Drääsdner“, „Lütt“ and „Lüüd“ are niche for an easy puzzle unless the target audience is explicitly dialect-focused.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to medium or use very widely recognized dialect markers only.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Umarmung beim Tor“ can express joy, but it is a very specific sports-context scene and can also be a general gesture of comfort or solidarity. It is weaker than the other joy clues.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer joy sign such as „Jubelruf“ or „Lachen vor Freude“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Böllerschuss“ can signal celebration in some contexts, but it can also be associated with alarm, tradition, hunting or military salutes. It is not an everyday unambiguous sign of joy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „Luftballons steigen“ or „Freudentanz“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Tür steht immer offen“ is more hospitality/openness than direct freigiebigkeit. It could also indicate friendliness or trust rather than generosity.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer giving expression such as „Teilt sein letztes Brot“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Pestmaske“ is commonly associated with early modern plague doctors, not the medieval period in a clean historical sense.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a more clearly medieval item such as „Ritterrüstung“, „Klosterchronik“ or „Lehnsbrief“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Schutznetz gespannt“ is avalanche protection infrastructure, not something that announces an avalanche. It does not match the category wording „Was Bergwetter ankündigt“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a warning sign such as „Risse in der Schneedecke“ or „Wumm-Geräusch im Hang“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Schneebruch im Wald“ is damage caused by snow load, not specifically an avalanche sign.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a direct avalanche-related clue.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Buttermilch gerührt“ is not a strong example of fermentation as a method in the same way as sauerkraut, bread dough and beer. It may read as churning/stirring dairy instead.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Joghurt angesetzt“ or „Kimchi im Glas“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Mut“ and „Entschlossenheit“ overlap conceptually in several clues. „Allein losgegangen“, „Kein Zurück mehr“, „Brücken abgebrochen“ and „Bis zum Ende durch“ all signal resolve/courage rather than cleanly separate categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more contrastive categories, or make „Mut“ physical/social courage and „Entschlossenheit“ decision-finality with clearer wording.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The science concepts are high-school or specialist knowledge rather than concrete easy-tier everyday categories, especially quantum physics and relativity clues.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Ehrlichkeit“ and „Loyalität“ overlap through promise-keeping and truthfulness. „Hält was er verspricht“ and „Steht zu seinem Wort“ can be read as honesty as much as loyalty.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Make loyalty clues more about allegiance/support, e.g. „Bleibt beim Team“ or „Verteidigt den Freund“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Tanzend erzählt“ points to the original sense of ballad as dance song, but in German school/literary genre context it is not a clear defining feature of the literary ballad and may mislead.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer ballad feature such as „Dramatische Handlung“ or „Strophenform“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Unerhörte Begebenheit“, „Falke als Symbol“, „Wilhelm Meister“ and „Grüner Heinrich“ are literary-curriculum clues, not easy everyday categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Alle fliehen sofort“ can describe a rational emergency evacuation as well as mass panic. It is not as diagnostic as the other mass-panic items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Nein heißt vielleicht“ can imply manipulativeness or pressure rather than healthy persistence. It is a poor fairness clue for „Beharrlichkeit“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Nach Rückschlag weiter“ or „Täglich neu versucht“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Satz zu Ende gesagt“ does not by itself express impatience; it could even signal patience or politeness. The intended phrase may be „Satz nicht zu Ende gehört“.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Change to „Satz nicht abgewartet“ or „Unterbricht sofort“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The puzzle has 17 items: the „Vertrauen“ group contains five entries, while a 4×4 puzzle requires exactly four items per group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Remove one item from „Vertrauen“ so the puzzle has exactly 16 items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Misstrauen“ and „Skepsis“ are closely overlapping concepts. „Immer nachgeprüft“, „Zeig mir den Beweis“, „Zweimal hingehört“ and „Quelle gefragt“ can all reasonably express skeptical verification.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more emotional distrust cues for „Misstrauen“ and evidence-checking cues for „Skepsis“, or replace one category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Wiener Klassik“, „Volksmusik“ and „Kammermusik“ are not parallel-exclusive categories: chamber music is a form/ensemble setting that can exist within classical music, including the Wiener Klassik.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Weather signs overlap between seasons: „Gewitter am Abend“ and „Gewitter nach Hitze“ are both summer-like thunderstorm clues, while „Erster Bodenfrost“ can also announce winter rather than only autumn.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Stellt sich tot“ is not a characteristic fox behavior; it is more associated with animals such as opossums or some prey species. This makes the Fuchs group unfair.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Schläft mit Augen auf“ is not a standard owl characteristic. Owls may appear watchful, but the clue is not reliable.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The sports technique terms are quite specialized for easy tier, especially „Gabel“, „Zugzwang“, „Parade quarte“, „Riposte“ and several bowling/kegeln phrases.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Bahn einölen“ is a bowling-lane maintenance idea, not a normal clue for German Kegeln in the same way as Kugel, Neun Kegel or Nachschub/Nachwurf.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Kuppel über dem Schiff“ and „Doppelturm an Fassade“ are not uniquely Barock and can appear in other church architecture styles. The Barock group is less clean than the Romanik/Gotik groups.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Herzklopfen, klingelt“ is malformed/awkward as an item. It reads like two fragments accidentally combined, not a clean clue.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Zuckertüte in der Hand“ is strongly associated with the first day of school, but it signals the event itself more than the emotion „Stolz“. It could also fit excitement.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several items have non-idiomatic German phrasing: „Seekrank am Reling“, „Karte ans Lenker“ and „Reiseführer gibt Mikro“ sound ungrammatical or clipped.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Schnittlinie anreißen“ is a measuring/marking action, not specific to a saw. It could belong broadly to many woodworking/tool tasks.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a saw-specific clue such as „Sägeblatt ansetzen“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Kerfdruck“ is not a common German easy-tier term and may be a mistranslation/technical oddity. It makes the Säge group less accessible.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „gleichmäßig sägen“ or „Sägespäne fallen“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Bit wechseln“ and „Kreuzklinge wechseln“ overlap because drill/driver bits and screwdriver tips are often interchangeable tool accessories. This can blur Bohrmaschine and Schraubenzieher.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Zeuge Jehovas“ as a doorbell visitor category is culturally recognizable but targets a religious group and can feel stereotyping compared with neutral occupational categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a neutral category such as „Nachbar“, „Spendensammler“ or „Umfrageperson“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Klemmbrett dabei“ is not specific to museums; teachers or guides may carry a clipboard on any class trip. It is a weak museum clue.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Vitrine betrachten“ or „Ausstellungsraum betreten“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Pausensekt für Lehrer“ is not a student/class-trip theater feature and could be read as adult event behavior rather than theater itself.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with „Sitzreihe suchen“ or „Applaus nach Schluss“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Beerdigt Gefährten“ is not a clear, established raven-specific intelligence behavior. It may be confused with general animal mourning or corvid gatherings around dead birds.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a better-known raven/corvid intelligence clue such as „Werkzeug gebogen“ or „Futter versteckt“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: „Malt mit Rüssel“ is a trained performance by captive elephants, not a natural sign of elephant intelligence. It is misleading as a category clue.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use „Erkennt sich im Spiegel“ or „Trauert am Kadaver“.

verdicts.gptExplanation: There are wording errors: „Carving am Blaupiste“ should be idiomatic German such as „Carving auf der blauen Piste“, and „Halftube“ should be „Halfpipe“.
