verdicts.gptExplanation: Passport and Boarding Pass are used at check-in but also at security screening and boarding. The Check-in Desk category is situational rather than exclusive.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several 'Famous Ancient Temples' are not cleanly ancient in the same sense as the other categories: Borobudur and Angkor Wat are medieval-era religious monuments, and Abu Simbel/Karnak overlap strongly with ancient Egyptian temple/wonder associations.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Focaccia is both a flatbread and a leavened bread, so it reasonably fits two listed groups. More broadly, 'Flatbreads' and 'Leavened Loaves' are not mutually exclusive bread categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Challah is an enriched bread, but it is not reliably a sweet bread; many standard challahs are only mildly sweet or savory table breads.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The puzzle relies on hobby-board-game taxonomy and specific modern game titles. For an easy puzzle, these are not concrete everyday categories for most players.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Pontoon bridges are floating bridges, not cable-supported bridges. Rope bridges may be suspended, but 'Cable-Supported Bridges' normally refers to cable-stayed and suspension-family structures, making this grouping imprecise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Pontoon Bridge to a floating-bridge group or replace it with Suspension Bridge.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tied Arch Bridge and Bowstring Arch Bridge are near-overlapping terms, and bowstring bridges are also a truss/arch hybrid. This makes the arch and beam/truss split less clean than an easy puzzle needs.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Most phobia names here are specialized Greek-derived terms. For easy, this is vocabulary-heavy and not concrete everyday knowledge.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Proof types and abstract mathematical classifications are not concrete everyday easy categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Observational learning and latent learning are learning types, not conditioning types in the same sense as classical and operant conditioning. The label overextends the category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Triangle and quadrilateral categories overlap with '3D Solids' only weakly through terms like tetrahedron and octahedron, but 'Prism' is a broad solid family that can include triangular or quadrilateral bases. The geometry taxonomy is not a single clean axis.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Carl Jung is associated with analytic psychology and psychoanalysis but diverged from Freud; Viktor Frankl and Rollo May are often classified as existential psychologists as well as humanistic psychologists. The labels are defensible but not clean for easy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more canonical examples for each school or move to a harder tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The puzzle uses abstract science terminology across physics, cosmology, and chemistry. It is more educational/medium than easy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use more concrete science examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cao Dai is a syncretic Vietnamese religion with Abrahamic, Buddhist, Taoist, and Confucian influences, not cleanly an East Asian religion in the same sense as Taoism, Confucianism, and Shinto. Indigenous traditions are practices/categories rather than specific religions, so category types also differ.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use specific religions/traditions on all four rows, or label the last group as 'Indigenous Religious Concepts' and avoid mixing it with named religions.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Mathematical constants and number categories/properties overlap conceptually because constants like pi, e, and Apery's constant are also numbers. The labels are distinguishable for solvers, but not strictly mutually exclusive.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a different non-number category or label the first group more narrowly.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Catalan numbers, Apery's constant, abundant/deficient numbers, and amicable numbers are specialized number theory terms for easy tier.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Many body-process terms are technical biology/medicine vocabulary, especially depolarization, granulation, remodelling, and epithelialisation. This is not easy-tier everyday material.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use more familiar body processes.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Clavicle is not normally classified as a thorax bone; it belongs to the pectoral girdle. Sacrum forms part of the pelvis but is axial skeleton rather than one of the pelvic girdle bones in the same sense as ilium, ischium, and pubis.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Clavicle with a rib-related term and either relabel the pelvic group or replace Sacrum with Acetabulum/Hip Bone terminology.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Fisherman's Knot and Double Fisherman's are commonly treated as bends, but they can also form loops or stopper-like knots depending on use. Monkey's Fist is often a weight knot as much as a stopper knot. This is knot-specialist taxonomy and not clean for easy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use only very canonical knot examples or move to a harder tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Blizzard is both a severe storm and fundamentally a wind/snow event, while sirocco is a named wind rather than a wind event. The weather labels mix phenomena, event types, and storm systems.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use cleaner labels such as 'Types of Wind', 'Forms of Precipitation', 'Optical Effects', and 'Storm Types'.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Charon is not usually a Roman god; he is the ferryman of the underworld from Greek mythology, adopted into Roman myth but not a god like Neptune or Pluto. Mercury is also not primarily a sky/state god; he is a messenger/trade/travel god.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Charon with a clearer underworld deity and move Mercury to a commerce/messenger grouping or replace him.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Temperate Rainforest appears under temperate forests, while Rainforest and Cloud Forest appear under tropical forests; rainforests/cloud forests can be tropical or temperate depending on location. Subalpine forest can overlap with boreal/montane zones, and riparian/flooded/swamp/gallery forests overlap by moisture regime.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use one classification axis, such as climate-zone forest types only, and avoid moisture/landform categories in the same puzzle.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Hybrid genres overlap with the other genre groups: dark comedy is a comedy, docudrama is a drama/nonfiction hybrid, noir can overlap with crime/drama, and psychological thriller can be action/drama depending on framing. The label is not parallel with the other subgenre labels.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use four parallel parent genres or four non-overlapping format categories.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Depth of Field is a camera/lens setting or optical result, not primarily a composition principle. Bracketing is a shooting technique/camera setting workflow, not a processing term. Vignette can be a lens effect or composition/framing effect as well as a processing term.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Depth of Field to settings/optics, Bracketing to shooting techniques, and use clearer composition/processing terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Lavender and mint can be both ornamental flowering plants and edible herbs. Sedum includes many species used as ground covers but is also a broad succulent genus, making it less precise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use plants that are more exclusively associated with one gardening role.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The categories overlap heavily. Ranked choice and single transferable vote are electoral systems/voting methods; runoff election appears both as a counting/tally method and as a special election type; primary and by-election are also election types. Players could reasonably place several items in multiple groups.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a single axis such as electoral systems only, election timing only, or institutions only.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Speculative fiction items such as dystopia, cyberpunk, and alternate history are narrative fiction modes/subgenres, so they overlap with 'Narrative Fiction Modes'. Satire and pastoral can also be prose/fiction modes as well as poetic/dramatic forms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use non-overlapping genre families or separate by medium/form more consistently.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Paisley is not usually a geometric repeat; it is an ornamental teardrop/boteh motif. Ikat and batik are dyeing/weaving techniques that produce many pattern types, not specifically pictorial prints.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Paisley to ornamental motifs and replace Ikat/Batik with clearly pictorial print types.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Plaid/check patterns are geometric repeats, so 'PLAID AND CHECK PATTERNS' overlaps conceptually with 'GEOMETRIC REPEATS'.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the second group to a narrower non-check geometric category or replace the plaid/check group.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Quartz watch and atomic clock are electronic timekeeping devices, but atomic clocks are clocks rather than timers. Sidereal time is a time standard/astronomical measure, not a calendar system.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename 'Electronic Timers' to 'Modern Timekeepers' and replace Sidereal Time with a true calendar system.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Specific scientific laws across physics, thermodynamics, electricity, genetics, chemistry, and population biology are too specialized for an easy puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Avogadro's Law is commonly a gas/chemistry law, while Law of Mass Action can be chemistry or biochemical kinetics. The 'biology and chemistry' label is broad and unlike the narrower physics labels, making the taxonomy uneven.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Split biology and chemistry into separate puzzles or use a narrower category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Famous shipwrecks are ships categorized by fate, while the other groups categorize ships by type/era/organization. A warship or exploration ship can also be a famous shipwreck, so the axis is mixed.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use parallel categories such as exploration ships, warships, ocean liners, and spacecraft, without a fate-based group.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Lusitania was sunk in wartime and can be associated with war history, which may blur it with the famous warships group despite being a passenger liner.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a shipwreck with less military/war association.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tempo markings have overlapping ranges and traditions vary. Larghetto can be faster than Largo and near Adagio; Andantino can mean slightly faster or slower than Andante; Allegretto sits between moderate and fast.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use broader groups or avoid adjacent tempo bands in an easy puzzle.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Schwarzbier is a dark lager, not a dark ale. Gose is usually a sour wheat beer but not normally grouped with Hefeweizen/Witbier/Berliner Weisse as a straightforward wheat-beer style for easy categorization.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Schwarzbier to lager styles or replace it with a true dark ale such as Brown Ale; consider replacing Gose with a more obvious wheat beer.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sumerian is usually treated as a civilization/city-state culture rather than an empire in the same way as Akkadian, Assyrian, and Babylonian. Greek is also broad and not one single Mediterranean empire.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer empires such as Hittite/Persian in the relevant regions, or relabel as 'Ancient Civilizations'.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Maya and Mississippian are generally civilizations/cultures rather than empires comparable to Aztec and Inca.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the puzzle/categories to 'Ancient Civilizations' or replace with more defensible empire examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sonnet, villanelle, and rondel are both rhyming and syllabic/metrical fixed forms in many traditions. Epic appears in both literary genre contexts and narrative poetry. The labels are understandable but not fully exclusive.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use one axis such as fixed forms, narrative forms, and modern forms, with clearer labels.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The W in 'Sword' is silent, but the listed silent letter is not initial like the other Silent W examples. This is still technically a silent W, but it breaks the obvious pattern and may feel like a trap in an easy puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Sword with Wreck, Write, Wren, or Wrinkle.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several equipment terms are specialized for easy tier, such as tennis dampener, net strap, ball hopper, archery nocking point, and bracer.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more common equipment items or move to medium.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Best Picture is not a directing or writing Oscar; it is awarded to producers and is the overall picture category.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Best Picture with a true directing/writing category or relabel the group to include Best Picture explicitly.

verdicts.gptExplanation: 'American Rivers' combines North and South America, while the other continent labels use single continents. It is understandable, but less parallel and can confuse players expecting North American vs South American categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to 'Rivers of the Americas' or split North and South America in a different puzzle.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Piqué can be a step used in turns, but 'Piqué Turn' is clear; Rond de Jambe is commonly a barre exercise but also a movement performed outside barre. The labels are mostly fair but not fully exclusive for ballet vocabulary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use only examples strongly tied to barre for the barre group, or move to a harder tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Vitruvian Man is a drawing, not a painting. The group label says Da Vinci paintings, so this is factually off.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with a Leonardo painting such as Salvator Mundi, The Annunciation, or Madonna of the Rocks.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Mulligan is not an official golf rules or penalty term; it is an informal do-over. Stroke Play is a scoring format, not a penalty/rules item like drop or hazard.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to 'Golf Rules and Formats' or replace with clearer penalty/rules terms such as Out of Bounds and Penalty Stroke.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The puzzle relies on specialized cricket vocabulary such as Doosra, Googly, Gully, and Mid-On. That is likely too domain-specific for an easy puzzle unless the pool expects cricket literacy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or replace with more globally familiar sports terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Egyptian deities often have overlapping domains, and several listed items can reasonably fit more than one category. Isis is strongly associated with magic as well as protection; Hathor has sky/solar associations; Neith can be associated with sky/creation/war/protection. This makes the grouping unfair.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use deities with single, widely recognized domains, or change the theme to more concrete labels such as symbols, animals, or famous myths.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sokar, Kherty, Heka, Seshat, Sia, and several domain distinctions are obscure for an easy puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use only the most familiar Egyptian gods with very broad categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Individual Medley is not a relay event; it is an individual swimming event.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Individual Medley with a true relay event such as 4x100 Freestyle Relay or 4x100 Medley Relay.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several Monopoly properties are assigned to the wrong color groups. Oriental Ave and Vermont Ave are light blue, not purple/brown; Pacific Ave and North Carolina Ave are green, not dark blue.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Mediterranean Ave, Baltic Ave, and two correctly named properties only if the intended group is not the standard U.S. Monopoly color set; otherwise make PURPLE/BROWN a 2-item concept impossible for this format and choose another group.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The ingredient/type categories overlap with sushi form categories. Shrimp Nigiri and Eel Nigiri are effectively ebi/unagi nigiri, while Ebi and Unagi are cooked sushi toppings that can appear as nigiri. Cooked sushi is not mutually exclusive with nigiri, maki, or temaki.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep all categories at the same level, e.g. sushi forms only, or toppings only; do not mix form and ingredient/cooked-status labels.

verdicts.gptExplanation: This is highly cuisine-specific for easy. Rajma Masala, Chettinad Masala, Rasam Powder, Idli Podi, tadka, and asafoetida are not everyday English easy-tier items for many players.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use broader, more familiar spice categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several entries in the prefix/suffix groups are also idiomatic expressions, which blurs the intended distinction. Firsthand, Backhand, Shorthand, and Underhand are not simply suffix compounds in the same way as Hand Saw; they are lexicalized words with figurative or specialized meanings.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer suffix compounds that are concrete nouns, or separate all entries by idiom versus compound without mixing lexicalized meanings.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Rapid Fire can be a compound ending in Fire and also an idiomatic/adjectival expression, while Fight Fire is usually only part of the longer idiom 'fight fire with fire'. The suffix and idiom categories are not cleanly separated.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Rapid Fire with a less idiomatic fire-ending compound and use complete idioms such as 'Fight Fire With Fire' if idioms are intended.

verdicts.gptExplanation: All four groups are about blended words or portmanteaus, but two labels use the same concept at different levels: 'Food Portmanteaus', 'Tech Portmanteaus', and 'Modern Culture Blends' are semantic-domain labels, while 'Animal Blends' is ambiguous between hybrid animals and blended words. The category basis shifts from word formation to biological hybrids.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename ANIMAL BLENDS to 'HYBRID ANIMALS' and avoid presenting it as parallel to portmanteau word groups, or make all groups word-blend categories.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Mule is a hybrid animal but not a portmanteau or lexical blend. It breaks the apparent word-blend theme.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Mule with a true animal-name blend such as Coywolf or Beefalo, if the intended category is blended animal names.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Swoosh is not specifically a water sound; it is a general movement/air sound and could fit impact or other sound categories depending on context. Splash is also impact-with-water, so WATER SOUNDS and IMPACT SOUNDS have some conceptual bleed.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Swoosh with a more water-specific sound such as Ripple or Babble.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Metroidvania is typically an action-adventure/platforming genre, so it overlaps with ACTION / COMBAT rather than being cleanly only narrative/adventure. Walking Simulator is also a loose, contested genre label.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer adventure labels such as Graphic Adventure, Text Adventure, Hidden Object, and Interactive Fiction.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The mushroom categories are not mutually exclusive. Oyster and Shiitake are prized edibles as well as supermarket mushrooms; Lion's Mane is edible and commonly sold; False Morel can be eaten in some traditions but is also toxic/misleading. Truffle is not a mushroom in the ordinary cap-and-stem sense, though it is a fungus.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use non-overlapping labels such as 'common grocery names', 'deadly/toxic names', 'medicinal supplement names', and 'luxury culinary fungi', with carefully chosen items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several cooking techniques cross the listed heat-medium labels. Baste and Render are actions rather than cooking methods parallel to Confit and Deep-Fry; Braise uses both moist and dry heat; Blanch can be preparatory rather than a cooking category. The categories are not clean single-concept buckets.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer technique families with parallel labels, or replace Baste/Render with unambiguous fat-cooking methods.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The bread-process categories mix phases, actions, and outcomes. Fold commonly happens during bulk proofing but is also a dough-handling/mixing action; Pre-Shape and Final Shape are shaping steps, not proofing; Bloom and Crust are results of baking, not baking actions; Rest can occur before proofing, after shaping, or after baking.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a chronological process with unambiguous stages, or rename groups to actions/results and choose items consistently.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Fermentation categories overlap by biochemical process. Kombucha and Tepache include alcoholic and acetic/lactic fermentation components; Soy Sauce, Miso, and Doenjang involve molds plus other microbial fermentation; Shrub is usually vinegar-based syrup rather than necessarily a fermented food itself.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use product-type categories rather than fermentation chemistry, or choose examples with one dominant, commonly taught fermentation type.

verdicts.gptExplanation: NVMe is a storage interface/protocol, not a storage device like SSD/HDD/eMMC. Cache and ROM can be memory, but cache is often part of processors and ROM is storage-like in ordinary computer terminology. PCIe and Thunderbolt are buses/interfaces rather than connectivity devices like NIC or USB Hub.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep categories parallel: hardware components only, protocols/interfaces only, or consumer-visible parts only.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The OSI-layer mapping is not clean. Bandwidth and Latency are cross-layer performance measures, not specifically physical-layer items; TLS is usually above transport/session/presentation rather than application in strict OSI terms; Gateway can be used generically across layers; Switches can operate beyond layer 2 in common usage.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use canonical OSI examples only, or avoid strict layer labels for easy-tier networking.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The surgical terminology is far beyond easy-tier everyday knowledge. Laparotomy, thoracotomy, sternotomy, hysteroscopy, palatoplasty, and transplant organ shorthand are specialized medical terms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a much harder tier or use broad everyday medical categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: IONISING RADIATION and NUCLEAR MEDICINE overlap conceptually. PET Scan uses ionising radiation and is also a nuclear medicine modality; SPECT, Scintigraphy, Bone Scan, and Thyroid Scan also involve ionising radiation.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Separate by imaging modality labels such as X-ray-based, MRI-based, ultrasound-based, and nuclear medicine, and move PET Scan to nuclear medicine.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: HIFU is a therapeutic ultrasound technique, not primarily an imaging method like the others in this puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace HIFU with Sonogram or Elastography.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Philosopher-school classification with names such as Wolff, Sidgwick, and Smart is too academic for easy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use more broadly recognized philosophy terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Chinese Room is usually a philosophy of mind / AI thought experiment, not metaphysics. Utility Monster is normally associated with ethics/utilitarianism, not political philosophy. Desert Island is too vague to identify a specific political-philosophy thought experiment.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Chinese Room to a mind/AI category, Utility Monster to ethics/utilitarianism, and replace Desert Island with a recognized political philosophy item.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several thought experiments span multiple philosophical subfields. Experience Machine and Utility Monster are closely tied to utilitarian ethics; Brain in a Vat and Evil Demon are epistemology but also metaphysics/mind-adjacent; Fission Case is personal identity/metaphysics but overlaps philosophy of mind.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more introductory and clearly field-specific examples, or raise the tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Artemis I was uncrewed, so it does not belong in CREWED EXPLORATION. Curiosity and Perseverance are rovers rather than landers. Cassini was primarily an orbiter/probe, not a flyby mission in the same sense as Voyager/New Horizons/Pioneer.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Artemis I with a crewed mission, rename ROBOTIC LANDERS to 'Mars/Titan Surface Missions' or use true landers, and rename Cassini's group to probes/orbiters.

verdicts.gptExplanation: War is usually a comparing/shedding-style children's card game rather than a banking game. Mahjong is commonly tile-based and not a rummy card game in ordinary English usage, even though it has matching/set collection elements.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace War with a clearer banking/casino game and Mahjong with a card-game item that is unambiguously rummy-like.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several Yiddish-derived words are assigned to categories they do not fit. Nosh means to snack/eat, not talk/socialize; Schmaltz means rendered fat or excessive sentimentality, not wisdom/praise; Schlep means to drag/carry or a tedious journey, not wisdom/praise; Schpiel means a speech/pitch, not praise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move Nosh to food/eating and replace the WISDOM / PRAISE group with accurately matched terms such as Maven, Mazel tov, Naches, and Kvell if appropriate.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Lox and Borscht are culturally associated with Jewish cuisine but are not Yiddish words in the same way as many of the other entries, so the theme basis is inconsistent.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Clarify the label as Jewish/Yiddish-associated food, or use food words with clearer Yiddish lexical status.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Taklamakan is an Asian desert, not a polar/cold desert in the same geographic grouping. Great Basin is American, not polar, though it is a cold desert. The POLAR / COLD label mixes climate and location while the other labels are continents.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep all groups geographic, moving Taklamakan to Asia and Great Basin to America, or make all groups climate/type-based.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Marie Curie won the Nobel Prize in Physics and Chemistry, so she belongs in Physics here, but Francis Crick won the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, not a separate 'Medicine Prize' in the common shorthand sense is acceptable. The larger issue is that Marie Curie also won Chemistry, creating possible cross-Nobel ambiguity if categories are strict prize fields beyond the listed ones.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Consider replacing Marie Curie with a Physics-only laureate to avoid cross-prize ambiguity.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Pu'u 'O'o is a volcanic cone/vent on Kilauea but is not usually classified as a standalone cinder cone volcano in the same way as Paricutin, Cerro Negro, or Sunset Crater. Lassen Peak is commonly described as a lava dome/plug dome, but Novarupta is a lava dome from an eruption rather than a volcano name parallel to the others.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use widely accepted examples for each volcano type.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Volcano morphology examples such as Cerro Negro, Pu'u 'O'o, Soufriere Hills, and Novarupta are specialized for easy-tier play.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use famous volcanoes grouped by continent/location.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Four Seasons is a set of violin concertos, but the item name is less formally parallel than the others and could be recognized as a general work title rather than a concerto title. Violin Concerto in D is ambiguous because multiple composers have famous violin concertos in D major.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use composer-qualified titles such as Vivaldi's Four Seasons and Tchaikovsky Violin Concerto, or choose less ambiguous concerto names.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Spring Sonata and Kreutzer Sonata are harder classical-music references for easy unless the target audience is expected to know classical repertoire nicknames.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to medium/hard or use better-known titles with composer names.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Dead Weight is not a standard high-camp logistics term in mountaineering parallel to Bivouac, Base Camp, and Cache.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Dead Weight with Load Carry, Depot, Camp I, or another recognized logistics term.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Base Camp is not usually a high camp; it is the staging camp below higher camps. Rotation and Rest Day are logistics/acclimatization schedule terms, while Altitude Sickness and Hypoxia are conditions rather than acclimatization actions.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename groups to separate conditions, schedule tactics, gear, and hazards, or choose items with matching grammatical roles.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The groups are continents, but 'THE AMERICAS' combines North and South America while the other groups are single continents. This is not inherently unfair, but it is less parallel than the rest.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename all labels as broad regions, or split Americas only if the puzzle format allows.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Some entries are not usually called wonders in the same sense as the ancient/modern-world-wonder landmarks, especially Eiffel Tower and Statue of Liberty depending on the intended list. The category title suggests 'wonders', but the groups are actually geographic landmark buckets.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Retitle to 'Famous Landmarks' or use only items from a specific recognized wonders list.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several contronym assignments are strained or ambiguous. Screen can mean show or conceal, not clearly allow/prohibit; Table has opposite meanings in different dialects but not allow/prohibit; Fix does not cleanly mean both separate and join; Trim can mean decorate/add or cut/remove but is context-heavy; Left means remaining or departed, not secured/free.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use well-established contronyms with category labels that match their actual opposing senses.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Contronyms grouped by subtle paired meanings are not easy-tier. Many require dictionary-level sense knowledge and dialect awareness.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or replace with concrete everyday categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Some examples can involve multiple memory systems. Swimming strokes are procedural but also overlap with semantic knowledge of stroke names; tracking a sentence can be working memory but also language comprehension; keeping score in mind could be working memory or semantic/procedural depending on task.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more prototypical examples with less cognitive overlap, or raise the tier.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cognitive psychology categories such as episodic, semantic, procedural, and working memory are academic for easy, even if the examples are concrete.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to medium or use everyday labels such as personal memories, facts, skills, and short-term mental tasks.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Euler's Formula” is not uniquely a geometry theorem; it commonly refers to the complex-analysis identity e^(ix)=cos x + i sin x, while the puzzle also has “Euler's Theorem” in another category. The near-identical Euler labels make the intended split unfair without more precise wording such as “Euler's Polyhedron Formula.”
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename “Euler's Formula” to “Euler's Polyhedron Formula” or replace it with a less overloaded geometry theorem.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Overture” is spelled OVER + TURE, not OVER + “Ture” as a standalone suffix fragment. The item casing also makes the fragment look like a word rather than the intended completion.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use “Ture” only if the game convention allows arbitrary letter fragments; otherwise replace it with a cleaner OVER completion such as “Time,” “Cast,” “Head,” or “Board.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several words do not hide the listed animal as a contiguous substring. “Locate” does not contain CAT; “Vacation” does not contain CAT; “Grateful” does not contain RAT; “Strategy” does not contain RAT; “Encoder” does not contain COD; “Sewer” and “Brewer” do not contain EWE.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace the non-matching words with words that contain the exact hidden animal substring, or relabel the mechanism if non-contiguous letters are intended.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Coral Snake” is not colour + snake in the same way as the other entries; “coral” is a noun/material/color term only indirectly, and the animal name is not normally parsed as a simple colour adjective plus snake. “Red-Bellied Black” is also incomplete as a standalone snake name unless “Snake” is implied.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use cleaner colour-adjective snake names such as “Green Anaconda,” “Brown Snake,” “Black Rat Snake,” or write “Red-Bellied Black Snake” in full.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Crab Nebula” is both commonly called a nebula and specifically a supernova remnant. Because “SUPERNOVA REMNANTS” is a separate category, this item reasonably belongs to two listed groups.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace “Crab Nebula” with a nebula that is not also primarily a supernova remnant, or avoid using “Supernova Remnants” as a parallel group.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Omega Centauri” is a globular cluster but is also widely discussed as the possible stripped core of a dwarf galaxy. In a puzzle that also includes “GALAXIES,” this creates a borderline ambiguity for astronomy-aware solvers.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Consider replacing it with an unambiguous star cluster such as “Hyades,” “M13,” or “Double Cluster.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: The equipment categories are functional rather than mutually exclusive. A condenser and rotary evaporator are commonly used in distillation/solvent removal, but a condenser is not itself separation equipment in the same direct way as a separating funnel; an aspirator is often a vacuum source and can also be treated as safety/utility equipment depending on context.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more direct separation items such as “Centrifuge,” “Chromatography Column,” “Filter Funnel,” and “Separating Funnel.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: Multiple items are misplaced or not Nahuatl loans. “Cocoa” is a food/plant product and overlaps with the FOOD category; “Chipotle” is food, not place/ritual; “Cenote” is from Yucatec Maya, not Nahuatl. “Mesquite” is generally from Nahuatl via Mexican Spanish, but the mixed “PLACE / RITUAL” group is not a coherent semantic category.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace the last group with a coherent Nahuatl-derived category, or move “Chipotle”/“Cocoa” into food and replace “Cenote” with a Nahuatl-derived place/ritual term.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Chili,” “Cocoa,” and “Chipotle” are all food terms; “Cocoa” also belongs with plant/product terms. This breaks the one-category-only requirement even apart from the etymology issue.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep food, animal, plant, and object/place categories strictly separated by replacing food/product overlaps.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The dice-game categories are not mutually exclusive. “Betting / banking,” “push your luck,” “matching / bluffing,” and “elimination” describe overlapping mechanics, and several games can reasonably fit more than one label.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use categories based on a single taxonomy axis, such as commercial dice games, traditional pub dice games, bluffing dice games, and roll-and-score dice games, or pick items with clearly exclusive mechanics.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Pig” and “Pass the Pigs” are push-your-luck games, but “Pig” is placed under ELIMINATION. “Liar's Dice” and “Perudo” are essentially the same bluffing family, while “Mexico” is also a bluffing dice game. These placements make the solution non-unique by mechanism.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move or replace “Pig,” and avoid using near-duplicate bluffing dice variants unless the category is explicitly about that family.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “P vs NP” is a computer science/mathematics problem, not an unsolved physics problem. “Consciousness” and “Why We Sleep” are interdisciplinary neuroscience questions rather than cleanly biology in the same sense as the other biology items, and “Fermi Paradox” is astrobiology/SETI rather than cosmology.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move “P vs NP” out of physics or replace it with a physics problem such as “Turbulence,” “High-Temperature Superconductivity,” or “Measurement Problem.” Consider tightening the other labels to avoid interdisciplinary overlap.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Record” belongs naturally to “Break a ritual” only if interpreted as a phrase, but “break a record” is not a ritual. It is a different sense of “break” from the group label and does not match the other ritual items.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace “Record” with a ritual/observance item such as “Sabbath,” “Vigil,” or “Tradition,” or relabel the group as general “things you can break by ending/interruption.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Draw” and “Fold” are card-game terms, but both are also fabric actions or fabric-related verbs in plausible senses: draw thread/fabric, fold fabric. This creates cross-category temptation beyond normal wordplay.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace one or both with card terms less viable as fabric actions, such as “Trump,” “Discard,” or “Ante.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Body” appears as an item in “Music anatomy” while “Body Text” appears in “Letter parts.” The puzzle title also cues body-part wordplay, but “body” is a legitimate part of a letter/document too, so the split relies on exact phrasing rather than clean semantic separation.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace “Body” in the music group with a more specific instrument part, or use “Soundboard” if the intended domain is string instruments.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Shadow types” and “Darkness terms” overlap conceptually: umbra/penumbra are darkness or partial-darkness regions, while eclipse is also an astronomical darkening event rather than simply a shadow type. “Twilight” and “Dusk” are low-light periods, not darkness terms in the same register as gloom/murk.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Make the shadow group strictly optical shadow terms and replace “Eclipse,” or relabel the darkness group as “Low-light conditions” with cleaner entries.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Simmer,” “Braise,” “Blanch,” and “Marinate” are cooking methods, not cooking times. The category label implies durations or timing stages, so the items do not fit as written.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel the group to “Cooking methods” or replace the items with actual timing terms such as “Rest,” “Proof,” “Bake Time,” and “Standing Time.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Discipline” and “Faculty” are academic institutions/roles as much as academic fields, while “Peer Review” and “Thesis” are academic-process terms. The category is internally loose compared with the other field senses.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Academic terms” or use field-specific terms such as “Discipline,” “Domain,” “Specialty,” and “Area.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Stack” and “Cache” are data structures/storage concepts, and “Boolean” is a type/value rather than program state. The group “Program state” is too broad and could absorb “Variable” but not the rest cleanly.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Programming terms” or replace with clearer state terms such as “Flag,” “Mode,” “Status,” and “Context.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: The category labels say “pair terms,” but most items are parts of a single eye, shoe, hand, or wing rather than terms for pairs. “Retina,” “Cornea,” “Insole,” “Knuckle,” and “Aileron” do not naturally denote pairs.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the labels to “Eye parts,” “Shoe parts,” “Hand parts,” and “Wing parts.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Torque,” “Thrust,” and “Momentum” are physical quantities but not power. “Logarithm” is not a mathematical power; it is the inverse operation related to exponentiation. These make the category labels inaccurate.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Exponentiation terms” and “Physics quantities,” or replace with actual power terms such as “Joule per Second,” “Kilowatt,” and “Work Rate.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Charger” and “Jumper Cable” are things used to revive a dead battery, not descriptors or states of a dead battery like “Drained” and “Flat.”
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Car battery context” or replace them with states such as “Spent,” “Discharged,” or “Flat.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Footstep” and “Eyebrow” are not standard dead metaphors. A dead metaphor is a figurative expression whose metaphorical force has faded; these words may be etymologically compound/body-part terms, but they are not good examples of dead metaphors.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer dead metaphors such as “Deadline,” “Broadcast,” “Skyscraper,” or “Chair leg,” depending on the intended standard.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The tennis group mixes actual fault terms with general tennis scoring/serve terms. “Let” and “Advantage” are tennis terms but not faults, so the label “Tennis fault” is misleading.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Tennis terms” or use four fault-specific terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Cartridge,” “Propellant,” “Report,” and “Recoil” are ammunition/firearm terms, but only “cartridge” can be a blank round. “Propellant,” “report,” and “recoil” are not blank ammunition types or components uniquely tied to blanks.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Relabel to “Blank round effects/components” or use “Blank Cartridge,” “Wad,” “Primer,” and “Case.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Fragile” and “Shatter” are literal properties/actions of glass as a material as well as metaphorical glass associations. They overlap with “Glass material” more than “Ceiling” and “Half Full” do.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with more clearly idiomatic metaphor entries such as “House,” “Cliff,” “Slipper,” or “Ceiling,” depending on the intended phrase set.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Parquet” is a type of flooring/building floor material, not specifically a dance floor. “Stage” can be a performance floor/area rather than a dance floor. These items overlap with the building-floor sense.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use dance-specific entries such as “Ballroom,” “Disco,” “Studio,” and “Club,” or relabel the group as performance/dance venues.

verdicts.gptExplanation: “Crash” and “Investigation” are associated with flight recorders after an accident but are not parts or alternate names of a black box. The group label is tighter than the items support.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with “Flight Data,” “Cockpit Voice,” “Recorder,” and “Beacon,” or relabel as “Air accident investigation terms.”

verdicts.gptExplanation: Teal is commonly treated as a blue-green color, so it can reasonably fit either Shades of BLUE or Shades of GREEN.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Teal with a less ambiguous blue shade such as Cerulean, Sapphire, or Indigo.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Dune is strongly associated with both beaches and deserts, and the puzzle also has a Desert category.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Dune in Beach with a more beach-specific item such as Tidepool, Lifeguard, or Seashell.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Lagos is not the capital of Nigeria; Abuja is. This makes the African Capitals group factually incorrect.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Lagos with Abuja or another African capital.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Blazer is more commonly categorized as a jacket rather than a coat, so placing it under Coats is debatable in a clothing taxonomy puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Blazer with a clearer coat type such as Overcoat, Duffle, or Raincoat.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Primer is more a material or preparatory coating than a painting tool, unlike Palette, Easel, and Brush.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Primer with a clearer tool such as Roller, Palette Knife, or Sponge.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Clay is a sculpting material, not a sculpting tool. It breaks the category logic alongside Chisel, Mallet, and Kiln.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Sculpting Materials/Equipment or replace Clay with a clearer tool such as Rasp or Loop Tool.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Aperture is a camera setting/opening, not a photography tool in the same sense as Tripod, Lens, and Shutter.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Aperture with Camera, Flash, Light Meter, or Filter.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Storm Events and Cold Weather overlap because blizzards, hail, sleet, and flurries are also storm or storm-like weather events. The categories do not define mutually exclusive domains.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer labels such as Thunderstorm Phenomena, Winter Precipitation, Heat Conditions, and Mild/Cloudy Conditions.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Drizzle and Overcast are not especially 'calm weather'; they are precipitation/cloud-cover conditions and could fit a broader weather-events category.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with calmer-condition words such as Stillness, Clear Skies, Calm, or Mild.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Snort is not a characteristic cow sound in the same way as moo, low, and bellow; many animals snort, including horses and pigs.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Snort with Calf-like 'Moo' alternatives such as Bleat is not ideal; better use 'Lowing' if distinct from Low, or replace the item set to avoid duplication.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tabloid is a newspaper format/type, not a newspaper section. This group mixes sections with non-sections.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Tabloid with Sports, Business, Opinion, Arts, or Weather.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Headline is an article element rather than a newspaper section, unlike Classifieds and Editorial.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Headline with a true section such as Sports, Local, Business, or Weather.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Pamphlet, brochure, leaflet, and flyer are very close near-synonyms. A group of near-duplicates is less satisfying and can feel arbitrary rather than categorical.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a more differentiated print-format set, such as Broadsheet, Tabloid, Zine, and Pamphlet.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Bolt can mean run away, so it fits Meaning ESCAPE, but it can also mean dash or sprint, overlapping with Meaning RUN.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Bolt with Escape-specific words such as Abscond, Elude, or Get Away.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Dodge can mean avoid rather than escape, and it can also describe a quick movement. It is less clean than the other ESCAPE synonyms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Dodge with Escape, Elude, Abscond, or Break Free.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Granita is itself a frozen dessert, so it overlaps with Frozen Desserts as well as Iced Drinks.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Granita in Iced Drinks with Frappuccino, Iced Tea, Iced Coffee, or Smoothie.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Daiquiri is a cocktail that may be served frozen, but it is not inherently an iced drink in the same way as Slushie or Frappe.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer iced drink item such as Iced Tea, Iced Coffee, Smoothie, or Slush.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Nickel appears as both a US denomination and a metal term inside Nickel Alloy, creating avoidable lexical overlap in a coin-themed puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Nickel Alloy with Copper-Nickel, Cupro-Nickel, Zinc, or Brass, or replace the denomination.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Whimper can be a quiet crying sound and also resembles the soft vocal category headed by WHISPER. This creates a weak overlap between CRY and WHISPER.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Whimper with Blubber, Bawl, or Tear Up.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The puzzle is for an English pool, but this set relies heavily on German terms with diacritics and less familiar food vocabulary. That may be beyond medium unless the theme is intentionally German and signposted.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Either retitle to explicitly signal German food/drink or replace the most German-specific terms with more familiar English names.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cut belongs strongly to both editing and on-set/directorial vocabulary, and appears in the same film-production semantic field as On Set.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Cut with a more specifically editing-oriented term such as Match Cut, J-Cut, L-Cut, or Crossfade.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Score can refer to music/sound but also broadly to film music, while 'Sound' also contains production sound terms like Foley and Boom. The group mixes sound recording and music categories.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Film Sound/Music or replace Score with a production-sound term such as Room Tone or Wild Track.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The astronomy vocabulary is significantly more specialized than typical medium, especially Apogee, Perigee, Eccentric, Retrograde, Barred, and Transit together.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or replace with more common astronomy terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Go terms such as Joseki, Komi, and Seki are specialized and likely too obscure for an English medium puzzle unless the puzzle is aimed at board-game enthusiasts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or use more broadly known game categories.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Bow can mean bend, but it is also close to a fold/curve action and may be read as a noun rather than a verb. It is less precise than the other BEND items.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Bow with Bend-specific verbs such as Crook, Warp, or Incline.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Wrench can mean twist, but it can also mean pull/strain violently and is a noun for a tool, making it less clean as a synonym category item.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Wrench with Twirl, Spiral, Torque, or Contort.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Platinum is not usually classified as a coinage metal in the traditional chemistry group; the coinage metals are copper, silver, gold, and roentgenium. Platinum is a precious/platinum-group metal.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Platinum with Roentgenium, or rename the group to Precious/Coin Metals and adjust the set.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Some is not a standard English article in the same core category as A, An, and The; it is usually a determiner/quantifier and can also function pronominally.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename Articles to Determiners or replace Some with another article only if the language set supports it.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sublime and Sublimity are essentially the same concept in different grammatical forms, appearing in the same group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace one with a distinct Romantic-period concept such as Nature, Individualism, Imagination, or Melancholy.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The literary/art-period concepts are fairly abstract and specialist for medium, especially when grouped by movements rather than surface vocabulary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Consider moving to hard or using more concrete, widely taught examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Architectural-period technical terms such as Tympanum, Tracery, Cartouche, Trompe l'Oeil, Pilotis, and Ribbon Window are specialist vocabulary for medium.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or use more commonly recognized architectural features.

verdicts.gptExplanation: This geology taxonomy is very classification-heavy for medium, especially distinctions like intrusive vs extrusive igneous and clastic sedimentary.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or simplify to broader rock categories with more familiar examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Many dance/music forms here are culturally specific and obscure for English medium, especially Danzón, Guaguancó, Forró, Maracatu, Chacarera, and Malambo.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Move to hard or replace with more widely recognized dance/music forms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Flamenco is Spanish/European, but the category European Origin is much broader than the other country-origin labels, making the taxonomy uneven and potentially too broad.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use parallel country/region labels throughout, such as Spanish Origin, Polish/Czech Origin, Italian Origin, etc., or broaden all labels by continent/region.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Heart is an organ that pumps blood, but it is not part of the transport vessel set in the same way as artery, vein, and capillary. The category label 'Transport System' is also vague for the circulatory system.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Circulatory System or replace Heart with Blood/Vessel depending on the intended category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Thymus, spleen, tonsils, and lymph nodes are lymphatic/immune organs, but 'Defense System' is an imprecise label and could also include skin, white blood cells, antibodies, etc.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Lymphatic/Immune Organs.

verdicts.gptExplanation: La Tomatina is a tomato-throwing festival, not a water festival. Holi Phera is also not a standard water-festival item; Holi is a color/spring festival, while water may be part of some celebrations.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with clearer water festivals such as Thingyan, Bon Om Touk, Boryeong Mud Festival, or Water Festival.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Oktoberfest is primarily a beer festival rather than a food festival, and La Mercè is a city festival rather than specifically a food festival.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearly food-specific festivals such as Pizzafest, Maine Lobster Festival, Salon du Chocolat, or Gilroy Garlic Festival.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Radiation is a heat-transfer mode, but it also directly relates to light/electromagnetic phenomena, creating overlap with Light Phenomena.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Keep Radiation only if the heat-transfer context is clearer, or replace it with Thermal Conductivity/Evaporation depending on the intended lesson.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Insulation is not a heat-transfer mechanism like conduction, convection, and radiation; it is a way of reducing heat transfer.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Insulation with Advection or rename the group to Heat/Temperature Concepts.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Voltage, resistance, and circuit are electrical concepts/components, not phenomena in the same sense as Static.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Electrical Terms or use phenomena such as Static, Lightning, Induction, and Arcing.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Rest, clef, and tempo are music notation/structure terms, but they are not musical bars. Measure is the only direct synonym for a bar.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Music Notation or replace items with terms directly related to measures/bars.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Mount and Dismount are gymnastics actions used beyond bars, not specifically gymnastics bars. The group mixes apparatus types with actions.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Gymnastics Terms or replace with High Bar and Low Bar if the intended category is apparatus.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Well and aquifer are groundwater sources/structures, not necessarily springs. They can be associated with water but are not the same category as geyser and hot spring.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Groundwater Sources or replace with Seep and Artesian Spring.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Vault belongs naturally in Spring jumps, but it also appears in gymnastics contexts where 'spring' can mean a springboard-assisted vault, creating possible thematic ambiguity with Trampoline.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more direct jump verbs such as Hop or Skip, and keep coil-spring objects clearly mechanical.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sharp and Natural are not flats in music; they are other accidentals. Key Signature can contain flats, but it is not a flat.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Music Notation or replace with B-flat, E-flat, A-flat, and D-flat.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Patch fits Flat tire, but also appears in the semantic space of fixing/repairing and is not a tire-specific item.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Sidewall, Tread, Valve Stem, or Spare if keeping the tire category.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Mesa and plateau are elevated landforms and not necessarily flat terrain in the same broad sense as prairie and steppe.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Flat/Elevated Landforms or replace with Plain and Savanna.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Voltage and resistor are electrical terms, but they are not electrical current. Ampere measures current; circuit can carry current.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Electrical Terms or replace with Direct Current, Alternating Current, Electron Flow, and Amperage.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sultana and Raisin are dried grapes, not currants in the strict sense. This can confuse solvers because the group is titled Currants.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Dried Grapes or replace with Blackcurrant, Redcurrant, Whitecurrant, and Zante Currant.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Trout, carp, salmon, and pike are fish that have scales, but the category label Fish scales suggests scale types or parts rather than fish species.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Scaled Fish or replace with scale-type terms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Legend appears under Map scale, but it is a map key item and overlaps with map-key terminology in similar puzzle themes. It is not specifically a map scale.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Legend with Representative Fraction, Scale Bar, Ratio, or Verbal Scale.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Contour and Grid are map features, not map scale terms. The group mixes general map-reading terms with scale-specific terms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Map Features or replace with scale-specific items.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Wick is associated with candles or lamps, not fire matches. It does not belong with Strike, Sulfur, and Flint.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Wick with Matchstick, Matchbox, Head, or Phosphorus.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sapphire, Cobalt, and Azure are blue color words, so they can fit a color-shades group rather than specifically Blue sea. The category label is not tight enough.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Shades of Blue or replace with sea-specific terms such as Reef, Current, Breaker, or Deep.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Canopy is not specifically a blue-sky item; it more often refers to trees, fabric coverings, or aircraft canopies.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Canopy with Firmament, Atmosphere, Cloudless, or Daylight.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Muddy is an indirect reference to Muddy Waters, which is more specialist than the other Blues music items and could be mistaken for a color/sea clue.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a clearer blues term such as Chicago, Shuffle, Harmonica, or Blue Note.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Precious metals, industrial metals, and alkali metals are all types or uses of metals, and Copper appears as both an industrial metal and a traditional coinage/precious-adjacent metal. The category domains are not mutually exclusive enough.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use mutually exclusive chemical families or clearly define the industrial category with non-overlapping examples.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Chorus, refrain, and hook are overlapping song-section concepts, and a bridge is itself another song section. The group is really song parts, not musical bridge.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Song Sections or replace with terms specifically related to bridging passages.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Nimbus is not normally a standalone cloud genus in the same way as Cumulus and Stratus; it appears in names like nimbostratus and cumulonimbus.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Nimbus with Cirrus, Altostratus, Nimbostratus, or Cumulonimbus.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Foam, steamed milk, and crema are coffee components, but 'Cloud in coffee' is an idiomatic/visual metaphor rather than a stable category. Latte Art is especially not a coffee cloud.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Coffee Foam/Milk Terms or replace with more direct 'cloud' senses.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tuber and bulb are not roots botanically; they are storage organs, typically modified stems or leaf bases. Rhizome is also a modified stem, not a root.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Underground Plant Parts or replace with root types such as Fibrous Root, Adventitious Root, Prop Root, and Taproot.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Etymology is the study/history of word origins, not a word root. Prefix and suffix are affixes, not roots.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Word Parts or replace with Root, Stem, Base, and Morpheme.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Canal, pulp, enamel, and dentin are tooth anatomy or dental terms, but only canal is directly tied to a root canal; enamel is on the crown, not the tooth root.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Tooth Anatomy or use root-specific terms such as Cementum, Apex, Canal, and Pulp Chamber.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Asphalt, resin, and creosote are related substances but are not simply pitch as tar. The group is chemically/materially loose.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Tar-like Materials or use Pitch, Tar, Bitumen, and Asphalt with a clearer label.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cunning can be a sharp mind trait, but it also implies slyness or deceit rather than intelligence alone, making it less clean than Astute, Shrewd, and Perceptive.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Cunning with Keen, Intelligent, or Insightful.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Crack and Snap can be sharp sounds, but they are also break verbs/sounds and could overlap with other common semantic groups. The group is somewhat loose.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more direct sound words such as Shriek, Screech, Whistle, or Yelp.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Stoic does not necessarily mean cold in personality; it means enduring pain/emotion without showing it. It overlaps only indirectly with aloof or detached.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Stoic with Frigid, Unfriendly, Remote, or Icy.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Slate, pewter, ash, and silver are greys rather than standard cool colors. The label Cool colours is too broad and subjective.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Gray Shades or replace with Blue, Green, Violet, and Teal.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Emotional weight and Weighty matters overlap conceptually: burden, responsibility, consequence, verdict, precedent, and mandate can all be serious or weighty matters.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Make one category specifically emotional feelings and the other specifically legal/civic authority terms, or replace overlapping abstractions.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: Organic can describe food/agriculture and is not inherently ecological or green in the same way as Renewable, Sustainable, and Carbon Neutral.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Organic with Eco-friendly, Low-carbon, Recyclable, or Conservation.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Covetous and resentful are adjectives while Jealousy and Grudge are nouns; the group mixes forms, and Grudge is not specifically envy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use consistent envy nouns/adjectives, such as Envious, Jealous, Covetous, and Resentful.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Treasury, Mint, and Federal Reserve are money institutions, not 'green as money' terms. The label points to the color idiom but the items are financial/government entities.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to US Money Institutions/Terms or replace with Cash, Dollars, Bills, and Greenbacks.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Drain and overflow are water-flow or plumbing terms, but they are not naturally 'running' in the same way as stream and faucet. Overflow can also be a common error term in software.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Brook, Creek, Current, or River.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Fly can be part of fly fishing, but it can also be a fly ball/verb/noun in other contexts. Within Fishing cast it is more lure type than casting item.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Fly with Cast, Line, Hook, or Bait, or rename to Fishing Gear.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sling and fracture are associated with injuries and support, but they are not medical casts. The group mixes cast materials/devices with related injury terms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Fracture Care or replace with Fiberglass, Plaster, Splint, and Brace.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Echo and resonance are sound phenomena, but not sound waves themselves in the same way amplitude and frequency are wave properties.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Sound Phenomena or replace with Wavelength and Period.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Trend, viral, movement, and uprising are not clearly 'social waves'; the phrase is nonstandard and the items are broad social-change concepts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Social Trends/Movements or replace with clearer wave idioms.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Lager, Pilsner, Pale Ale, and Session are beer styles that may be light in body/color/alcohol, but they are not all necessarily 'light beer'.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Beer Styles or use explicit light-beer terms such as Lite, Low-Cal, Session, and Radler.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Signature can fit Musical key as key signature, but the word alone is broad and also relates to documents/identity. It is less clean than the other music actions/concepts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Key Signature as the item or replace with Tonic, Mode, or Scale.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Scale Bar is a map scale term, not a map key term. This overlaps with scale/map-scale categories and weakens the Map key group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Scale Bar with Legend Entry, Color Code, Icon, or Label.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Band can mean a ring of jewelry, but it also means a music group and a strip/loop generally. In a puzzle with sound/circus/boxing ring senses, it is a broad item.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Band with Wedding Ring, Solitaire, Signet, or Claddagh.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Trapeze, tightrope, and acrobat are circus acts/performers, not specifically circus ring elements. The group label is too narrow.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Circus Terms or replace with Big Top, Center Ring, Ringmaster, and Ring Curb.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Proofread is an editing action, not a written draft. It does not belong cleanly with Manuscript, Outline, and Revision.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Proofread with Rough Draft, Version, Draft Copy, or Manuscript Page.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Pint and pour are associated with beer service generally, not specifically draft beer. The category mixes container/service words with draft system terms.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Beer Service or replace with Faucet, Taproom, Cask, or Draft Line.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Draught is just a spelling variant of draft and is too close to the category keyword rather than a distinct air-draft item.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Draught with Breeze, Current, Vent, or Airflow.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Shore is more general than river bank and can refer to lakes or seas. Floodplain is adjacent to a river bank but not itself the bank.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Riparian Zone, Riverbank, Berm, or Bank.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Type is too generic for Blood bank; it likely means blood type, but as a standalone item it can fit many domains, including data and document contexts.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Blood Type or Crossmatch.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Page can be a royal court attendant, but it is also a common book/page term and broad noun, making it less clean than the other royal-court items.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Page with Courtier, Noble, Jester, or Lady-in-waiting.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Club moss is not a moss; it is a lycophyte. Peat is a soil/accumulated organic material, not a moss or lichen, though it is often formed from sphagnum.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with true mosses/lichens such as Haircap Moss, Cushion Moss, Iceland Moss, or Usnea.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Chives are often treated as an herb culinarily but botanically are an allium/vegetable; this is acceptable in kitchens but a little fuzzy in a plant taxonomy puzzle.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a less debatable herb such as Parsley, Sage, Oregano, or Thyme.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Watching TV, birdwatching, watching time, and spectating all describe acts of watching or observation, so the category labels are intentionally punny but conceptually broad and partly overlapping.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Tighten labels to TV Terms, Birdwatching Gear/Activity, Timepieces, and Spectator Sports Terms.

labelOverlap.note: no lexical overlap — semantic-only claim; needs-claude-review

verdicts.gptExplanation: In Net finance, Profit and Income are very close because “net income” is essentially profit. Revenue is also commonly contrasted with profit but can be used in “net revenue,” making the set feel like overlapping accounting terms rather than four distinct net-finance items.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more distinct “net ___” finance phrases, or avoid placing Profit and Income together.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sand Trap is not a type of golf iron or an iron-related club term; it is a course hazard. Fairway is also a course area, not a golf iron. This makes the Golf irons group unfair.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with actual iron/club terms such as 3-Iron, 7-Iron, Long Iron, Short Iron, or Blade.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Concrete (definite) and Concrete evidence are conceptually nested: concrete evidence is a specific application of concrete meaning tangible/specific/verifiable. The item Verifiable especially bridges the two labels.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Change one group to a non-overlapping sense of “concrete,” or replace the evidence group with a clearer separate phrase.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tower can reasonably belong to Cell phone as “cell tower,” but it can also be a prison watchtower in the prison setting. Because Prison cell is another listed category, this creates avoidable ambiguity.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use a more phone-specific item such as Carrier, Handset, SIM, or Voicemail.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Parole is associated with prisons and criminal justice, but it is not a feature of a prison cell. Warden is also prison-related rather than cell-specific, weakening the category.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use cell-specific items such as Bunk, Bars, Lock, Toilet, or Solitary.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Emboss is not primarily a rubber-stamp item; embossing creates a raised impression and is closer to seal/notarial processes than a standard rubber stamp. It may pull toward Wax seal or official certification associations.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace with Handle, Rubber, Ink, Impression, or Date Stamp.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Range is the common term for a mountain chain, but it is also a generic ordered sequence or extent and can read as a chain-like series. More importantly, Summit, Ridge, and Pass are mountain features rather than mountain-chain terms, making the category less precise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer mountain-chain items such as Himalayas, Andes, Alps, Rockies, or Cordillera.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Launch belongs naturally to Rocket stage as well as Development stage. The rocket category already includes rocket-launch concepts, so this item can reasonably be selected with the rocket group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Launch in Development stage with Pilot, MVP, Alpha, or Release Candidate.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Trail is very close to the title theme Track record and can also fit running/railway contexts as a physical path. In Tracking evidence it is valid, but not uniquely anchored.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Trace, Log, Timestamp, or Breadcrumb instead.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cider is not wine, and Vineyard and Barrel are wine-production associations rather than a wine press itself. The Press (wine) group is semantically loose and one item is placed incorrectly.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Press (beverage making) or replace with Pomace, Crush, Must, Winepress.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Gallery and Canvas can belong to Picture frame, but they are broader art-display/art-object terms and not frame-specific. Canvas can also relate to film/storyboard visual media, making it less uniquely tied to the intended group.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Rabbet, Glazing, Backing Board, Moulding, or Hanging Wire for the picture-frame group.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Schooner is not a bark/barque; it is a different type of sailing vessel. Vessel is also generic rather than specifically a bark ship.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Barque, Three-Master, Foremast, Mainmast, Mizzenmast, or Square-Rigged.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Growl and Howl are dog vocalizations, but they are not specifically barks. Because the category is Dog's bark rather than Dog sounds, these are imprecise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the group to Dog sounds or use Bark-specific items such as Yap, Ruff, Woof, Arf.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Strike can mean a lightning strike, but it is also a general action in sports, labor, or combat. With Hardware bolt present, Wrench is similarly hardware-generic rather than bolt-specific, making the solve rely on broad association.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more bolt-specific terms such as Hex Head, Shank, Carriage, or Anchor Bolt, and lightning-specific terms such as Forked, Arc, or Discharge.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Crease and Bowler are central cricket terms but not specific to a cricket bat. Dugout and Home Run are baseball-game terms rather than baseball-bat terms. The two bat-sport groups are close enough that generic sport terms weaken uniqueness.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Make the labels broader, such as Baseball and Cricket, or use bat-specific items like Barrel, Knob, Handle, Willow, Blade, and Grip.

verdicts.gptExplanation: These four emotion categories are intensity levels or related affective states, not cleanly separated meanings. Afraid contains Terrified/Petrified while Furious contains Enraged, and the puzzle is mostly synonym sorting by subtle intensity, which is brittle for medium.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use clearer non-overlapping synonym sets or make the title/difficulty reflect a synonym ladder with carefully separated intensities.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Decadent and Indulgent can describe wealth/luxury as well as flavor, so they can pull toward Rich (wealthy). Saturated and Vivid are color terms, but Deep Red and Jewel Tone are examples rather than synonyms for a deep tone.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more flavor-specific items such as Full-Bodied, Savory, Umami, Heavy, and more tone-specific adjectives rather than examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Sleeve Art in Record Jacket overlaps with Book Jacket because jackets and sleeves are both cover-art packaging domains. Blurb and Synopsis are near-duplicates in Book Jacket, and both describe summary copy.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Replace Synopsis with Spine or Back Cover, and use record-specific items such as Inner Sleeve, Label, Catalog Number, or Gatefold.

verdicts.gptExplanation: The organ-system labels are biologically parallel, but several items are not organs despite the title Read the organs. Neuron, Synapse, Alveoli, Artery, Vein, Aorta, and Capillary are cells/structures/vessels, not organs. This is a title/category precision issue rather than a solve blocker.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename the title/category framing to body-system parts, or use actual organs consistently.

labelOverlap.note: labels share tokens but neither is subset — could be parallel-sibling (usually OK)

verdicts.gptExplanation: Tilt, Zoom, and Dolly are camera movements but not pans. They are parallel camera operations, so the label Camera Pan is too narrow.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Camera Moves or replace with Pan-specific items such as Left, Right, Sweep, Horizontal.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Several items are not cleanly “styles” of the named garment: Oven Mitt is a type of mitt rather than a glove style, Tool Belt is a functional belt, and Bandana/Cravat/Shawl can be neckwear but not necessarily scarf styles. The groups are solvable but the labels overpromise.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename groups to Hat Types, Handwear, Belt Types, and Neckwear, or use stricter style examples.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Heave Ho, Ahoy, Avast, and All Hands are nautical expressions/calls, but not all are specifically sailing commands. Ahoy is a greeting, and All Hands is a muster call.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Rename to Nautical Calls or replace with commands like Tack, Jibe, Trim, Reef.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Cell belongs naturally to Police Station, but it also strongly evokes prison cell from nearby puzzle vocabulary and is not station-specific. Antenna and Transmitter can also fit Communication channel-style categories in this batch, though within this puzzle they are only in Broadcast Station.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use Holding Cell for police, and consider more station-specific broadcast items such as Control Room or Call Sign.

verdicts.gptExplanation: Wonder, anticipation, and pride are broad feelings, and several items can cross categories: Excitement can be anticipation or wonder, Triumph can be pride or an event outcome, and Amazement/Astonishment overlap with Awe/Marvel very tightly. This is mostly a synonym-sorting puzzle with fuzzy boundaries.
verdicts.gptSuggestedFix: Use more sharply separated emotional states or replace cross-cutting words like Excitement and Triumph.
